message:
feat(storage,cli): incremental blocked-cache updates, bulk cycle-check adjacency loading, and CLI output refinements

Storage (sqlite.rs):
- Add incremental blocked-cache update support via cache_affected_ids field
  on MutationContext: when only specific issues change, recompute just their
  cache entries instead of rebuilding the entire blocked_issues_cache table
- Add invalidate_cache_for() method to merge affected issue IDs across
  multiple mutation operations within a single commit
- Replace per-node SQL queries in cycle detection with bulk-loaded in-memory
  adjacency list (load_cycle_check_adjacency), reducing cycle check from
  O(V * query_latency) to O(V+E) with a single SQL query

Config (mod.rs):
- Enhance configuration module with additional validation and option handling

CLI Commands:
- Refactor orphans command output mode detection to use args.robot flag
  directly instead of checking is_json/is_toon/robot separately, improving
  output mode precedence logic
- Add TOON output support to ready and show commands
- Minor README documentation update

Tests:
- Add new E2E tests for orphans, ready, and routing commands
- Extend e2e_env_overrides with additional TOON format validation
- Update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/
 
 ## Why This Project Exists
 
-I (Jeffrey Emanuel) LOVE [Steve Yegge's Beads project](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads). Discovering it and seeing how well it worked together with my [MCP Agent Mail](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp-agent-mail) was a truly transformative moment in my development workflows and professional life. This quickly also led to [beads_viewer (bv)](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer), which added another layer of analysis to beads that gives swarms of agents the insight into what beads they should work on next to de-bottleneck the development process and increase velocity. I'm very grateful for finding beads when I did and to Steve for making it.
+I (Jeffrey Emanuel) LOVE [Steve Yegge's Beads project](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads). Discovering it and seeing how well it worked together with my [MCP Agent Mail](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail) was a truly transformative moment in my development workflows and professional life. This quickly also led to [beads_viewer (bv)](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer), which added another layer of analysis to beads that gives swarms of agents the insight into what beads they should work on next to de-bottleneck the development process and increase velocity. I'm very grateful for finding beads when I did and to Steve for making it.
 
 At this point, my [Agent Flywheel](http://agent-flywheel.com/tldr) System is built around beads operating in a specific way. As Steve continues evolving beads toward [GasTown](https://github.com/steveyegge/gastown) and beyond, our use cases have naturally diverged. The hybrid SQLite + JSONL-git architecture that I built my tooling around (and independently mirrored in MCP Agent Mail) is being replaced with approaches better suited to Steve's vision.
